root_cause: A transient cluster network partition caused DNS and Kubernetes API reachability to fail before connectivity recovered
evidence: Timeline shows 1 recovered cluster-network outage episode(s) for container 'controller' within 20 minutes
DNS failure to 'leader-elector.default.svc.cluster.local' is followed by Kubernetes API reachability failure to 'kubernetes.default.svc:443' before recovery
Representative recovery signal: Controller reconnected to cluster network and readiness probe succeeded for container "controller"
Container 'controller' is currently ready=True, which is consistent with a recovered partition rather than a steady outage
Both kube-dns and kubernetes services still had ready endpoints during the incident window, which makes a single backend outage less likely

causes.message: The workload depends on both cluster DNS and Kubernetes API reachability to function normally
DNS and Kubernetes API traffic failed in the same short outage window, which is more consistent with a transient cluster network partition than a single service-specific outage
Connectivity later recovered without a configuration change, indicating the outage was transient
The workload experienced a temporary cluster-network partition and then recovered

pod:controller-manager-5f8669bd77-s7kpm: The pod lost both DNS and Kubernetes API reachability before recovering
container:controller: DNS lookup failed for leader-elector.default.svc.cluster.local: lookup leader-elector.default.svc.cluster.local on 10.96.0.10:53: read udp 10.244.1.33:49817->10.96.0.10:53: i/o timeout
Failed to reach Kubernetes API: Get "https://kubernetes.default.svc:443/api/v1/namespaces/default/configmaps?limit=1": dial tcp 10.96.0.1:443: i/o timeout
Controller reconnected to cluster network and readiness probe succeeded for container "controller"
service:kube-dns: CoreDNS service still had ready endpoints during the outage episode
service:kubernetes: Kubernetes API service still had ready endpoints during the outage episode

likely_causes: Overlay or CNI networking temporarily partitioned the pod from core cluster services
Node-to-service routing or conntrack state briefly broke reachability to both CoreDNS and the Kubernetes API
A transient control-plane network interruption isolated workloads from internal service VIPs before routes recovered
suggested_checks: Inspect CoreDNS and apiserver connectivity metrics around the outage window
Check node, kube-proxy, and CNI telemetry for packet drops, route reprogramming, or conntrack churn
Compare whether other pods on the same node also lost DNS and Kubernetes API reachability during the same interval
